mapping.a_element: nonequilibrium entropy limiters that control spurious oscillations and artificial dissipation in lattice Boltzmann methods (LBM)
mapping.b_element: theoretical approaches (macroscopic, mesoscopic, microscopic) that model elastic interactions and phase separation dynamics in alloys

mapping.a_element: entropy "trimming" and entropy filtering as mechanisms to restrict or filter nonequilibrium entropy
mapping.b_element: elastic interactions (e.g., lattice misfit, modulus differences) that influence phase separation kinetics and morphology

mapping.a_element: control of entropy production and artificial dissipation in LBM
mapping.b_element: control of precipitate shapes and arrangements via elastic anisotropy or external stresses in alloys

brief_justification: While both papers address mechanisms to control numerical or physical artifacts (spurious oscillations in LBM vs. elastic misfit in alloys), their underlying machinery differs fundamentally. Paper A focuses on entropy-based numerical limiters to stabilize simulations, whereas Paper B models phase separation driven by elastic interactions and continuum/atomistic theories. No shared governing equation or process unifies them.
